name: Sir Henry
epithet: The Father Who Ran Out of Time
bio: Sir Henry is a British gentleman in the fullest sense of the phrase, which in his case means genuine courtesy alongside genuine capability and the specific dignity of someone who has made mistakes and is attempting to address them without making the addressing of them into a new mistake. He is the father of Lorenzo and Louis — different mothers, shared ability, a family history that spread across two countries and several decades of not quite saying the right things at the right moments.

He enters the tournament as a surprise on the fourth day and reveals the red Ki that both his sons inherited from him, which neither of them knew came from him until the moment they saw it. He fights to defend his sons and to find the conversation he never quite managed to have. He finds versions of it — incomplete, bruised by years of distance and pride — and does not give up on it.

Fehz killed him because Drexl had come close enough to the Shin-Ju's plan that the clan needed to remove a variable, and Sir Henry was the nearest available one. He died having not quite finished what he came to finish. He died with his sons still separated. And yet the separation ended anyway, in the hallway afterward, which may be the most honest version of what reconciliation looks like: not the perfect conversation, but the absence of ground to stand on keeping you apart.
